claims.statement: HaloBank's solar null-geodesic diagnostic is only admissible at diagnostic tier when a pinned weak-field solar metric context reproduces the canonical solar-limb deflection scale and remains within configured gamma residual envelopes for historical light-deflection and Cassini-era Shapiro benchmarks.

constraints: metric context must resolve to the pinned weak-field BCRS/GCRS manifest
solar-limb deflection uses the Sun monopole weak-field benchmark
explicit source directions must remain outside the apparent solar disk for the chosen observer and epoch
body-fixed observer support is restricted to loaded orientation models and otherwise fails deterministically
Shapiro comparison remains a gamma residual benchmark and not a spacecraft-specific replay

sources.citation: IAU 2000 Resolution B1.3, relativistic framework for BCRS and GCRS.
sources.equation: weak-field metric written in terms of scalar/vector potentials for BCRS/GCRS

claims.notes: This claim stays diagnostic and benchmark-oriented; it does not assert a certified light-propagation solver.

claims.statement: HaloBank's inner-solar weak-field metric parity claim is only admissible at diagnostic tier when the same pinned solar metric layer satisfies both the Mercury perihelion benchmark and the solar null-geodesic benchmark inside configured deterministic tolerance bands.

constraints: Mercury precession and solar light-deflection modules must share the pinned metric model id
timelike and null benchmark residuals must both pass configured thresholds
claim remains reduced-order and benchmark-driven rather than certified

sources.citation: Einstein-era Mercury perihelion and light-deflection weak-field benchmark lineage.
sources.note: Timelike and null probes from the same solar weak-field source term.
Pinned shared metric model id and source-potential contract.

claims.notes: This parity node is a falsifiable consistency check tying two observable families to one metric layer.

claims.statement: HaloBank's Earth-Moon eclipse timing diagnostic is only admissible at diagnostic tier when apparent Sun-Moon contact minima are computed from an explicit Earth-referenced observer model and any unsupported body-fixed observer request fails deterministically with the route-matched orientation-kernel fail id.

constraints: observer semantics must remain Earth geocenter or supported Earth body-fixed geometry
unsupported body-fixed observers must degrade to deterministic geocenter fallback and fail the gate
event windows remain threshold-gated contact minima rather than certified eclipse catalogs

sources.note: Deterministic eclipse timing implementation and observer fail semantics.
TREE/DAG claim linkage for derived solar modules.

claims.notes: This claim stays diagnostic and observer-contract oriented; it does not assert a certified eclipse ephemeris.

claims.statement: HaloBank's Jovian moon event timing diagnostic is only admissible at diagnostic tier when transit and occultation minima are computed from an explicit Earth-referenced observer model and any unsupported body-fixed observer request fails deterministically with the route-matched orientation-kernel fail id.

constraints: observer semantics must remain Earth geocenter or supported Earth body-fixed geometry
unsupported body-fixed observers must degrade to deterministic geocenter fallback and fail the gate
event windows remain line-of-sight geometry minima rather than certified satellite ephemerides

sources.note: Deterministic Jovian event timing implementation and observer fail semantics.
TREE/DAG claim linkage for derived solar modules.

claims.notes: This claim stays diagnostic and observer-contract oriented; it does not assert a certified Jovian moon event catalog.

claims.statement: The active HaloBank local-rest solar peculiar-motion anchor is only admissible as a diagnostic reference when its runtime U,V,W vector matches a pinned published reference set within configured component residual tolerance.

constraints: published reference set must be pinned in repo manifest
runtime vector must resolve from configured solar-motion selector
residual check is deterministic and tolerance-gated

sources.citation: Schönrich, Binney & Dehnen (2010), MNRAS 403(4):1829-1833, Local kinematics and the local standard of rest.
Huang et al. (2015), MNRAS 449(1):162-174, Determination of the local standard of rest using the LSS-GAC DR1.

sources.note: Deterministic calibration gate and artifact generation.

claims.notes: This is a provenance and residual-consistency claim, not a certified Galactic dynamics claim.
